subject: Company Law
module_title: Transactions with Directors and Connected Persons
lesson_title: S239 CA 2014 — Loans, Quasi-Loans, and Credit Transactions with Directors

questions.question: S239 CA 2014 prohibits a company from making certain financial arrangements with directors and connected persons. Which THREE types of arrangement are caught by S239?
questions.explanation: S239 CA 2014 prohibits companies from making (i) loans, (ii) quasi-loans, and (iii) credit transactions to or for the benefit of directors or persons connected with directors. A 'loan' is the advance of money; a 'quasi-loan' is where the company pays a third party on the director's behalf expecting reimbursement; a 'credit transaction' is where the company supplies goods or services on deferred payment terms to a director. All three are caught by S239.

questions.question: Briam Ltd pays a personal credit card bill of €8,000 for its director, Noel, on the understanding that Noel will repay the company within three months. Under S239 CA 2014, what type of financial arrangement does this constitute?

options.A: A loan — because money changed hands
options.B: A quasi-loan — the company discharges a financial obligation of Noel's expecting reimbursement from Noel
options.C: A credit transaction — the company is extending credit to Noel for goods or services
options.D: This arrangement is not within S239 as it involves payment to a third party (the credit card company) not to Noel directly

questions.explanation: A 'quasi-loan' under S239 CA 2014 arises where a company pays a financial obligation of a director (or connected person) on the understanding that the director will reimburse the company. Briam Ltd paying Noel's credit card bill is a classic quasi-loan — the company is not paying Noel directly (it is not a loan) but it is discharging his liability to a third party. The expectation of reimbursement from Noel confirms the quasi-loan character.

questions.question: TechSpark Ltd supplies computer equipment worth €15,000 to its director, Paula, on 12-month deferred payment terms — the same terms offered to commercial customers. Does this transaction fall within S239 CA 2014?

options.A: No — S239 does not apply to ordinary commercial transactions
options.B: Yes — this is a 'credit transaction' under S239, which catches supply of goods or services on deferred payment terms to a director, regardless of whether the same terms are offered commercially
options.C: No — only transactions with a value above €10,000 are caught by S239
options.D: Yes — but only if Paula holds more than 50% of the company's shares

questions.explanation: A 'credit transaction' under S239 CA 2014 includes supplying goods or services to a director on terms that defer payment or otherwise extend credit. The fact that the terms offered to Paula are the same as ordinary commercial terms does not take the transaction outside S239 — the restriction applies to the category of person (director/connected person), not to whether the terms are commercially standard. S239 contains limited exceptions (discussed in subsequent questions).

questions.question: The general prohibition in S239 CA 2014 is subject to several exemptions. Which of the following is a recognised exception that permits a company to advance money to a director without breaching S239?

options.A: A loan approved by a special resolution of shareholders
options.B: A loan of an amount not exceeding €10,000 in aggregate (the 'small loan' exception)
options.C: Any loan advanced by a holding company to a director of a subsidiary company
options.D: A loan approved by two-thirds of the board of directors

questions.explanation: S239 CA 2014 contains several exceptions: (i) loans not exceeding €10,000 in aggregate (the de minimis exception); (ii) loans, quasi-loans, and credit transactions by a company to its holding company or subsidiary (group inter-company transactions); (iii) expenditure incurred by the director on the company's business (where the company meets the expense directly); and (iv) transactions in the ordinary course of business on commercially reasonable terms. The small loan threshold is a common exam trap — only amounts at or below €10,000 fall within the exception.

questions.question: The S239 CA 2014 prohibition applies to loans to directors and 'connected persons'. Under S220 CA 2014, which of the following is NOT a connected person relative to a director?

options.B: A company in which the director holds shares carrying more than one-third of the voting power
options.D: A former colleague who is a friend of the director but has no corporate or family relationship

questions.explanation: S220 CA 2014 defines connected persons as: a spouse or civil partner; children (including step-children and adopted children); parents; siblings; a trustee of a relevant trust; a body corporate in which the director holds shares carrying more than one-third of the voting power; and partners in a firm. A former colleague who is merely a friend with no corporate, family, trust, or partnership relationship is not a connected person under S220.

questions.question: MedLabs Ltd makes an unauthorised loan of €50,000 to its managing director, Dr Quaid, in breach of S239 CA 2014. Under S246 CA 2014, what is the legal status of this loan agreement?

options.A: The loan is void ab initio — Dr Quaid has no obligation to repay
options.B: The loan agreement is voidable at the company's election — the company may rescind it and require repayment of the principal; Dr Quaid remains liable to account for any benefit received
options.C: The loan is automatically converted into a gift upon discovery of the S239 breach
options.D: The loan is valid and enforceable — the remedy is confined to a criminal prosecution of the director under S248

questions.explanation: S246 CA 2014 provides that a loan, quasi-loan, or credit transaction made in breach of S239 is voidable at the company's election. Voidability means the company can elect to rescind the transaction and recover the principal (and any benefit) from the director. Crucially, even where rescission is sought, Dr Quaid remains liable to indemnify the company for any loss caused by the breach. The voidability operates in favour of the company — it cannot be used by the director to avoid repaying the money.

questions.question: The company in the previous question decides not to rescind the loan but seeks to hold Dr Quaid criminally liable for the S239 breach. Under what provision is he exposed to criminal liability and what is the maximum penalty?

options.A: S722 CA 2014 — fraudulent trading — Category 1 offence: up to 10 years imprisonment and/or €500,000 fine
options.B: S248 CA 2014 — breach of S239 is a Category 2 offence: up to 5 years imprisonment and/or €50,000 fine on indictment
options.C: S228 CA 2014 — breach of fiduciary duty — civil remedy only, no criminal liability
options.D: S599 CA 2014 — reckless trading — up to 3 years imprisonment and €250,000 fine

questions.explanation: S248 CA 2014 provides that a breach of S239 (loans, quasi-loans, and credit transactions to directors) is a Category 2 offence under the CA 2014 classification system. Category 2 offences attract: on indictment, a maximum of 5 years imprisonment and/or a fine of up to €50,000; on summary conviction, a lesser penalty. This is a relatively serious criminal sanction reflecting the policy importance of the prohibition.

questions.question: In Ruby Property Company v Kilty [1999] IEHC, the Irish High Court considered the relationship between S239 (loans to directors) and S246 (voidability). What aspect of the decision is most significant for understanding S239?

options.A: The court held that a company cannot recover a S239 loan if it was advanced more than 12 months before the action was commenced
options.B: The court confirmed that the voidability under S246 is a right of the company — not the director — and that a director cannot use their own S239 breach to resist repayment or convert the loan into a gift
options.C: The court held that S239 only applies to loans made from company funds and not from the proceeds of a credit facility
options.D: The court restricted S239 to loans to executive directors only, excluding non-executive directors

questions.explanation: Ruby Property Company v Kilty [1999] IEHC is significant for confirming that the right of rescission under S246 is a right vested in the company (for the benefit of shareholders and creditors) — not a right of the director. A director cannot invoke S246 to avoid repaying a S239 loan. The voidability protects the company from being bound by an unauthorised transaction; it does not release the director from their obligation to repay money received from the company in breach of S239.

questions.question: In Re Inch View Ltd [2022] IEHC, the Irish High Court found that loans had been made from the company to its director without disclosure or authorisation. What consequence did this have for the director's S819 restriction application?

options.A: The loans were considered irrelevant to the S819 restriction proceedings as they were a separate civil matter
options.B: The undisclosed S239 loans were treated as relevant conduct weighing heavily against the director in the S819 restriction proceedings — concealing financial transactions from the company weighed against a finding of acting responsibly under the La Moselle test
options.C: The loans automatically triggered a mandatory disqualification order under S839 CA 2014
options.D: The court held that S239 breaches cannot be considered in S819 restriction proceedings as they involve different statutory provisions

questions.explanation: In Re Inch View Ltd [2022] IEHC, the High Court found that the director had arranged undisclosed loans from the company to himself, and that this conduct was directly relevant in S819 restriction proceedings. The La Moselle five-fold test requires a director to demonstrate they acted 'honestly and responsibly' in relation to the company's affairs. Concealing S239 loans from the company's officers and records was a significant factor in the court finding that the director had not acted responsibly, resulting in a restriction order.

questions.question: PassionFoods Ltd is a wholly-owned subsidiary of KitchenGroup Ltd. KitchenGroup instructs PassionFoods to advance a €200,000 loan to KitchenGroup's managing director, Mr Burke. Mr Burke is also a director of PassionFoods. Does S239 apply?

options.A: No — S239 does not apply to intra-group transactions
options.B: Yes — Mr Burke is a director of PassionFoods, so any loan from PassionFoods to him (regardless of who instructs it) is within S239
options.C: No — because the instruction comes from KitchenGroup (the parent), PassionFoods is merely acting as agent and S239 does not apply
options.D: Yes — but only if the loan is not repaid within 12 months

questions.explanation: S239 CA 2014 prohibits companies from making loans to their directors. The prohibition is based on the relationship between the lender (PassionFoods) and the borrower (Mr Burke). Mr Burke is a director of PassionFoods — it is irrelevant that KitchenGroup instructed the loan. The fact that he is also MD of the parent company does not change the analysis. The intra-group exception (S239(7)) applies to loans between group companies — not loans from a company to an individual director.
